entry.title: Cloud Custodian - AWS CLI
entry.content:
CLI Entry Point is : c7n/cli.py

CLI name: custodian

CLI main options: custodian run custodian schema # https://github.com/cloud-custodian/cloud-custodian/blob/master/c7n/schema.py#L641 custodian report custodian version # https://github.com/cloud-custodian/cloud-custodian/blob/master/c7n/version.py custodian validate

Sun Nov 15 01:05:37 IST 2020

Lets discuss more about custodian schema sub-command today.

$>custodian schema  #or# $>custodian schema aws/azure/gcp cloud provider name can be used based on the type of custodian tool installed
- aws.account
- aws.acm-certificate
- aws.alarm
- aws.ami
- aws.app-elb
- aws.app-elb-target-group
- aws.artifact-domain
- aws.artifact-repo
- aws.asg
- aws.backup-plan
- aws.backup-vault
- aws.batch-compute
- aws.batch-definition
- aws.cache-cluster
- aws.cache-snapshot
- aws.cache-subnet-group
- aws.catalog-portfolio
- aws.cfn
- aws.cloud-directory
- aws.cloudhsm-cluster
- aws.cloudsearch
- aws.cloudtrail
- aws.codebuild
- aws.codecommit
- aws.codepipeline
- aws.config-recorder
- aws.config-rule
- aws.customer-gateway
- aws.datapipeline
- aws.dax
- aws.directconnect
- aws.directory
- aws.distribution
- aws.dlm-policy
- aws.dms-endpoint
- aws.dms-instance
- aws.dynamodb-backup
- aws.dynamodb-stream

- aws.ebs

- aws.ec2

- aws.ecs

- aws.elasticsearch-reserved
- aws.elb
- aws.emr

- aws.iam-certificate

- aws.s3

- aws.sagemaker-job
- aws.sagemaker-model
- aws.sagemaker-notebook
- aws.sagemaker-transform-job

- aws.security-group
- aws.serverless-app
- aws.shield-attack
- aws.shield-protection
- aws.simpledb
- aws.snowball
- aws.snowball-cluster
- aws.sns
- aws.sns-subscription
- aws.sqs
- aws.ssm-activation
- aws.ssm-managed-instance
- aws.ssm-parameter
- aws.step-machine
- aws.storage-gateway
- aws.streaming-distribution
- aws.subnet
- aws.support-case
- aws.transit-attachment
- aws.transit-gateway
- aws.user-pool
- aws.vpc
- aws.vpc-endpoint
- aws.vpn-connection
- aws.vpn-gateway
- aws.waf
- aws.waf-regional
- aws.workspaces

For the record the number of resources listed are 180 as of Sun Nov 15 01:20:03 IST 2020



$> bash common_actions_across_resources.sh
      1 attach-encrypt
      1 auto-patch
      1 autorecover-alarm
    115 auto-tag-user
      2 backup
      1 configure-lifecycle
      1 configure-lifecycle-policy
      4 copy
      1 copy-cluster-tags
      1 copy-instance-tags
    115 copy-related-tag
    100 delete
      1 delete-bucket-notification
      1 delete-global-grants
      1 delete-integration
      2 deregister
      1 detach
      3 disable
      1 disable-s3-logging
      1 enable-cloudtrail
      1 enable-data-events
      1 enable-s3-logging
      1 enable-vpc-routing
      1 encrypt
      1 encrypt-instance-volumes
      1 encryption-policy
      1 encrypt-keys
      1 encrypt-s3-destination
    180 invoke-lambda
    180 invoke-sfn
    110 mark-for-op
      4 modify
      1 modify-db
      1 modify-db-cluster
      1 modify-endpoint
      1 modify-instance
      1 modify-listener
      2 modify-policy
     10 modify-security-groups
      1 no-op
     25 normalize-tag
    180 notify
      1 patch
      1 pause
    180 post-finding
    180 post-item
      1 propagate-spot-tags
      1 propagate-tags
    180 put-metric
      1 reboot
      1 region-copy
      2 release
      1 remove-keys
      1 remove-launch-permissions
      1 remove-permissions
      1 remove-shared-accounts
     10 remove-statements
    115 remove-tag
      1 remove-website-hosting
     26 rename-tag
      1 request-limit-increase
      3 resize
      1 restore
      2 resume
      4 retention
      1 revoke-access
      2 send-command
      3 set-attributes
      2 set-boundary
      1 set-bucket-encryption
      1 set-concurrency
      1 set-continuous-backup
      1 set-ebs-encryption
      1 set-emr-block-public-access
      4 set-encryption
      3 set-flow-log
      1 set-groups
      1 set-immutability
      1 set-instance-profile
      1 set-inventory
      1 set-lifecycle
      2 set-logging
      1 set-metadata-access
      2 set-monitoring
      1 set-password-policy
      1 set-permissions
      1 set-policy
      1 set-protection
      1 set-protocols
      2 set-public-access
      1 set-public-block
      1 set-query-logging
      1 set-replication
      1 set-retention-period
      1 set-rotation
      1 set-s3-logging
      1 set-s3-public-block
      1 set-scanning
      7 set-shield
      1 set-shield-advanced
      1 set-snapshot-copy-tags
      1 set-ssl-listener-policy
      1 set-state
      1 set-statements
      1 set-stream
      2 set-waf
      1 set-xray-encrypt
      6 snapshot
      4 start
      8 stop
      1 suspend
    115 tag
     28 tag-trim
      3 terminate
      1 toggle-logging
      1 toggle-versioning
      5 update
      1 update-agent
      1 update-cluster
      1 update-config
      1 update-environment
      1 update-integration
      1 update-method
      1 update-trail
      1 upgrade
    180 webhook
$>cat common_actions_across_resources.sh
#!/bin/bash

cat /dev/null > actions_list.txt
for resource in `custodian schema aws | grep ^- | awk '{print $NF}'`
do
    custodian schema ${resource}.actions | grep "\- " | awk '{print $NF}' >> actions_list.txt
done
cat actions_list.txt | sort | uniq -c
$>


$> bash common_filters_across_resources.sh
      1 access-analyzer
      1 access-key
      6 age
      2 attributes
      1 bucket-encryption
      1 bucket-logging
      1 bucket-notification
      1 capacity-delta
      1 check-cloudtrail
      1 check-config
      6 check-permissions
      1 check-public-block
    132 config-compliance
      1 connection-status
      1 continuous-backup
      2 credential
     26 cross-account
      1 data-events
      1 db-parameter
      1 default-ebs-encryption
      7 default-vpc
      1 dhcp-options
      1 diff
      2 distribution-config
      1 ebs
      1 egress
      1 emr-block-public-access
      1 ephemeral
    180 event
      1 event-source
      1 fault-tolerant
    165 finding
      3 flow-logs
      1 global-grants
      2 glue-security-config
      1 grant-count
      1 group
      1 guard-duty
      1 has-allow-all
      3 has-inline-policy
      1 has-specific-managed-policy
      1 has-statement
      1 has-users
      1 has-virtual-mfa
      2 healthcheck-protocol-mismatch
     14 health-event
      6 iam-analyzer
      1 iam-summary
      2 image
      3 image-age
      1 ingress
      2 instance
      1 instance-age
      1 instance-attribute
      1 instance-uptime
      1 internet-gateway
      1 invalid
      1 inventory
      2 is-logging
      1 is-log-target
      2 is-not-logging
      1 is-shadow
      1 is-ssl
      1 key-rotation-status
      2 kms-alias
     17 kms-key
      1 last-write
      1 latest
      1 launch-config
      1 lifecycle-policy
      1 lifecycle-rule
      1 listener
      1 logging
    110 marked-for-op
     40 metrics
      1 mfa-device
      1 mismatch-s3-origin
      1 missing
      1 missing-policy-statement
      1 missing-route
      1 modifyable
      1 nat-gateway
     11 network-location
      1 no-encryption-statement
      1 no-specific-managed-policy
      1 not-encrypted
      5 offhour
      6 onhour
    180 ops-item
      1 param
      1 password-policy
      1 policy
      1 progagated-tags
      1 query-logging-enabled
    180 reduce
      1 reserved-concurrency
      1 rest-integration
      1 rest-method
      1 route
      1 s3-cidr
      1 s3-public-block
      4 security-config
     25 security-group
      1 securityhub
      1 service-limit
      8 shield-enabled
      2 shield-metrics
      1 singleton
      1 skip-ami-snapshots
      1 ssl-policy
      1 ssm
      1 ssm-compliance
      1 stale
      1 state-age
      2 status
     29 subnet
     51 tag-count
      3 taggable
      1 target-group
      2 task-definition
      1 termination-protected
      9 unused
      1 upgrade-available
      4 usage
      4 used
      2 user-data
      1 valid
    180 value
     14 vpc
      1 vpc-attributes
      2 vpc-endpoint
      1 vpc-id
      2 waf-enabled
      1 xray-encrypt-key
$> cat common_filters_across_resources.sh
#!/bin/bash

cat /dev/null > filters_list.txt
for resource in `custodian schema aws | grep ^- | awk '{print $NF}'`
do
    custodian schema ${resource}.filters | grep "\- " | awk '{print $NF}' >> filters_list.txt
done
cat filters_list.txt | sort | uniq -c
$>

Few actions are common for all the aws resources

  • invoke-lambda

  • invoke-sfn

  • notify

  • post-finding

  • post-item

  • put-metric

  • webhook

Few filters are common for all the aws resources

  • event

  • ops-item

  • reduce

  • value

Sun Nov 15 16:01:45 IST 2020

Vocabularies of Custodian

  • Resources

  • Actions

  • Filters

  • Modes

Summary of the custodian schema command outputs


$>custodian schema --summary
aws:
 resource count: 179
 actions: 126
 filters: 134
aliases:
 count: 2
mode:
 count: 11
$>

When custodian command is invoked it first calls c7n/cli.py

When custodian schema command first invoked, it first calls schema_cmd function in c7n/commands.py.

In schema_cmd function c7n/schema.py invoke happens through from c7n import schema

In schema_cmd function we have definition of all the allowed options for custodian schema

    # Here are the formats for what we accept:
    # - No argument
    #   - List all available RESOURCES
    # - PROVIDER
    #   - List all available RESOURCES for supplied PROVIDER
    # - RESOURCE
    #   - List all available actions and filters for supplied RESOURCE
    # - MODE
    #   - List all available MODES
    # - RESOURCE.actions
    #   - List all available actions for supplied RESOURCE
    # - RESOURCE.actions.ACTION
    #   - Show class doc string and schema for supplied action
    # - RESOURCE.filters
    #   - List all available filters for supplied RESOURCE
    # - RESOURCE.filters.FILTER
    #   - Show class doc string and schema for supplied filter
